verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70-1) is a canonically cytosolic/nuclear inducible heat shock protein with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, there is well-documented surface display on stressed and tumor cells (membrane-bound Hsp70 / mHsp70), where it binds outer-leaflet phosphatidylserine and Gb3 lipids and is recognized by clinical agents (e.g., cmHsp70.1 antibody, TKD-peptide-based NK therapies, PENAO/peptide imaging). This is a stress- and oncogenic-state-induced ecto-pool, qualifying as cell_state_induced. Not yes (no intrinsic anchor at baseline), not no (ecto-Hsp70 is a recognized tumor-restricted surface target).
